mappings.rationaleAI: Covers cell membrane structure (A.1), compartmentalization (A.2), and transport mechanisms including osmosis (A.3) and active transport (A.5).
Directly addresses the nerve impulse (C), neuron structure (C.1), synaptic transmission (C.4), ion channels, and bioelectricity (B).
Covers the molecular basis of membrane structure (A.1), molecular recognition in transport (A.4-A.5), and chemistry underlying bioelectricity (B).
Addresses the biochemical basis of membrane transport (A.4-A.5), chemical reactions in nerve impulse propagation (C.3), and muscle contraction (D).
Covers muscle contraction including striated, cardiac, and smooth muscle (D.2-D.4), nerve impulse transmission (C), and physiological mechanisms.
Covers neural signal processing, synapse function (C.4), and brain imaging of nerve impulse transmission (C).
Illustrates nerve impulse transmission (C) through photoreceptors, optic nerve signaling, and retinal processing of stimuli.
Covers contractile and motile activity across animal groups (D.1), including muscle systems and nervous systems in diverse phyla.
Relevant to bioluminescence (E) covering the range and variety of bioluminescent marine organisms (E.2).
